records.morph: [imp. & obs. p. p. interwove (?); p. p. interwoven (?); p. pr. & vb. n. interweaving.]

defs: To weave together; to intermix or unite in texture or construction; to intertwine; as, threads of silk and cotton interwoven.
quotes: Under the hospitable covert nigh Of trees thick interwoven.

defs: To intermingle; to unite intimately; to connect closely; as, to interweave truth with falsehood.
quotes: Words interwove with sighs found out their way.
